content: Curiosity-driven intrinsic motivation mechanisms (from the agent domain) can act as a dynamic pruning and prioritization gate for long-term memory in state-of-the-art 2024-2025 active learning agents, resolving the bottleneck of memory bloat that degrades active sampling performance. This framework creates a self-reinforcing loop where only high-curiosity, uncertainty-rich data is retained in long-term memory to drive more efficient active learning.
